data.title: Via Renewables reports $463.5M revenue and $2.70 EPS in 2025 10-K
data.description:
Via Renewables reported total revenues of $463.5 million for fiscal 2025, a 16% increase driven by higher volumes and a larger customer book, and reported net income per share of $2.70 (basic/diluted). Net income declined to $35.6 million from $61.1 million in 2024, primarily reflecting higher retail cost of revenues and lower segment margins. The company noted operating income of $53.5 million and a gross profit of $141.6 million, with tax expense of $10.5 million for the year.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $463.5 million, up $64.6 million (16%) year-over-year.
  • Gross Profit: $141.6 million, decreased versus 2024 primarily due to higher retail cost of revenues.
  • Operating Income: $53.5 million, down from $84.2 million in 2024 reflecting higher cost of revenues and lower segment margins.
  • Net Income: $35.6 million, down from $61.1 million in 2024; tax expense was $10.5 million.
  • Net Income Per Share (Basic/Diluted): $2.70, which reflects dividends on Series A preferred stock of $9.1 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Growth was driven by a larger customer book and higher electricity unit revenue.
  • Customer Expansion: Retail Customer Equivalents (RCEs) increased to 421,000, a net increase of 33,000 year-over-year comprised of approximately 188,000 organic additions and 46,600 acquired RCEs; annual attrition was about 4.2% in 2025.
  • Channel & Product Mix: Electricity accounted for roughly 67% of retail revenue in 2025, with emphasis on fixed and variable pricing products and green offerings.
  • Operational Transactions: The company acquired up to 3,300 RCEs in October 2025 and redeemed portions of its Series A preferred stock in late 2025 and early 2026.
  • Margin & Hedging Dynamics: Retail gross margin improved to $149.8 million; results and cash flows remain materially affected by hedging, derivatives, and weather variability.

Original SEC Filing: Via Renewables, Inc. [ VIASP ] - 10-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Inuvo 2025 10-K: Revenue $86.2M, EPS not provided; net loss $(5.1)M
data.description:
Inuvo reported fiscal 2025 net revenue of $86.2 million, a 2.9% increase year-over-year, while recording a net loss of $5.1 million; earnings per share were not provided in the filing. Gross profit was disclosed at $64.2 million with a 74.5% gross margin, down from 85.6% the prior year. The company noted changes in revenue mix and platform concentration as key drivers of results.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Revenue: $86.2 million, up 2.9% vs. 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $64.2 million; gross margin 74.5% (prior year 85.6%).
  • Net Income: $(5.1) million (net loss for the year).
  • Operating Income: Not explicitly stated in the filing.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not provided in Part II Item 7/Item 8 and omitted.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and channel concentration: Platform channel expanded to 83.8% of revenue, with two large platform clients continuing to dominate sales; one client increased spend aided by a new product while another reduced activity.
  • Product launches and AI capabilities: Introduced IntentPath and Ranger to expand AI offerings (visualization, QA/compliance) and enhance advertiser value.
  • New offerings and positioning: Launched IntentKey AI and a 25-million-concept graph aimed at agentic media planning and real-time intent-based targeting.
  • Go-to-market and personnel: Appointed a new chief operating officer to accelerate go-to-market for IntentKey and expanded agency and brand relationships with 15 new signings.
  • Operational outlook: Company focus is on scaling AI-driven products and leveraging platform relationships to drive future growth while managing concentration risk.

Original SEC Filing: Inuvo, Inc. [ INUV ] - 10-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: CarParts 10-K: $547.5M Revenue, $50.4M Net Loss
data.description:
CarParts reported fiscal year net sales of $547.5 million, a 7.0% decline versus the prior year largely due to a rationalization of marketing spend focused on profitability. The company recorded a net loss of $50.4 million, compared with a $40.6 million loss in the prior year, while gross profit fell to $179.3 million with a 32.8% gross margin. Operating actions including headcount reductions and fulfillment changes were taken to reduce operating expenses.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $547,525,000 (down 7.0% vs prior year)
  • Gross profit: $179.3 million (decreased 8.8% vs prior year); gross margin 32.8% (down 60 bps)
  • Loss from operations: $(48.7) million (presented as (8.9)% of net sales); operating expense 41.7% of sales
  • Net loss: $(50.4) million (compared to $(40.6) million prior year)
  • Net loss per share: Metric not provided in Part 2 Item 7/Item 8 and thus omitted

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Net sales declined as marketing spend was rationalized to prioritize profitability.
  • Channel mix: Continued emphasis on eCommerce channels (website, app, marketplaces) while wholesale/offline channels serve commercial customers.
  • Product breadth: Maintains approximately 1.66 million SKUs across replacement, hard and specialty parts to support broad vehicle coverage.
  • Operational actions: Implemented headcount reductions and fulfillment changes; recorded a $3.69 million impairment on long‑lived assets.
  • Market positioning: Leveraging SKU breadth, an aging U.S. vehicle fleet, and online growth to capture DIY and online demand.

Original SEC Filing: CarParts.com, Inc. [ PRTS ] - 10-K - Mar. 05, 2026

data.title: Victoria's Secret Sales Recover But Shares Fall on Concerns Over Expenses — WSJ
data.description:
By Suzanne Kapner and Kelly Cloonan

Victoria's Secret's chief executive said her strategy for reviving the retailer is working, though concerns over tariffs, charges related to Adore Me and higher expenses sent shares lower.

The bra and apparel retailer reported strong fiscal fourth-quarter results Thursday, beating Wall Street's estimates on both its top and bottom lines. CEO Hillary Super said in an interview that Victoria's Secret bra business grew for the first time in four years, its PINK brand had its best growth in a decade and the company gained market share.

The company's guidance for the fiscal year and current quarter also topped analysts' projections.

However, Victoria's Secret is facing pressure from several sources. It is projecting $160 million in added tariff costs for 2026. It expects to mitigate most of that through raising prices, offering promotions and working with vendors on costs for a net tariff impact of about $40 million.

"We are entering 2026 with momentum despite an uncertain macro environment," Chief Financial and Operating Officer Scott Sekella said during a call with analysts. "We remain focused on managing costs while continuing to invest in product innovation, brand strength and the customer experience."

The company also is contending with headwinds related to its acquisition of online intimates brand Adore Me in 2022. The company recorded a $119.6 million impairment charge and a $36.3 million charge related to the restructuring of a fulfillment center, both tied to Adore Me, in its latest quarter. It also said its starting a strategic review of its DailyLook clothing subscription business, which came with the Adore Me acquisition, as it looks to focus more on its core offerings.

Victoria's Secret also said it expects a selling, general and administrative expenses rate of 33% in the fiscal first quarter, up slightly from the year-ago period, due to investments in store labor.

The company also said it is spending more on marketing this year, building on the record results it saw from a Valentine's Day campaign with K-pop group TWICE. "In relation to marketing and what we have planned, the team is just getting started," Super said during a call with analysts. "We are moving quickly."

The stock slid 15% to $50.82 on Thursday. Shares have more than doubled over the past year.

For the fiscal fourth quarter, shoppers responded well to Victoria's Secret product and marketing in the quarter, as its customer base grew and it sold items at a higher average price, the company said.

Those trends pushed sales up 8% in the fiscal fourth quarter, with strength across brands, channels and geographies.

Victoria's Secret also exceeded its own financial guidance for sales and earnings despite an $85 million headwind related to tariffs, Super said. She welcomed Wednesday's ruling by a New York federal judge that may pave the way for companies to collect refunds on tariffs struck down by the Supreme Court.

"It would show the true value of what we've accomplished, because what we've accomplished has been slightly overshadowed by tariffs," she said in an interview. She added that Victoria's Secret didn't pass tariff-related costs onto consumers.

The company posted a fiscal fourth-quarter profit of $184 million, or $2.14 a share, compared with $193 million, or $2.33 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were $2.77, compared with estimates of $2.52 a share according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Sales rose to $2.27 billion from $2.11 billion a year earlier, and topped analyst estimates of $2.23 billion.

Same-store sales rose 8%, compared with analyst forecasts of 5.6% growth.

The retailer expects its growth to continue this year, pointing to a strong pipeline of products and marketing plans.

For the full year, Victoria's Secret guided for sales of $6.85 billion to $6.95 billion, or up about 5% to 6%. Analysts were looking for $6.77 billion in sales.

For the current quarter, Victoria's Secret forecasts sales of $1.49 billion to $1.53 billion, or up about 10% to 13%. Analysts forecast sales of $1.42 billion.

Super said she is still having conversations with Barrington Capital Group and BBRC International, two activist investors that have called for changes, including a board shake-up.

"We need the time and resources to focus on our strategic plan without a lot of outside distraction," she said.

Write to Suzanne Kapner at suzanne.kapner@wsj.com and Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Nike to Record $300 Million Charge From Cost-Cutting Efforts — Update
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Nike expects to record a charge of $300 million related to cost-cutting efforts, including job eliminations.

The charge is primarily associated with employee severance costs, most of which were recognized in its current quarter, Nike said in a Thursday filing.

Management approved in late February a plan to implement certain organizational changes as part of its review of ways to run the company more efficiently and profitably, the sneaker company said.

In January, Nike said it was laying off about 775 workers as part of its turnaround strategy. The cuts were expected to affect workers at Nike's distribution centers in Tennessee and Mississippi, amounting to about 1% of total workforce.

Chief Executive Elliott Hill told investors in December that Nike was still in the "middle innings" of its turnaround plan, as costs from investments in the business were continuing to squeeze margins.

Nike said Thursday it is continuing to evaluate opportunities to operate more efficiently and profitably by realigning costs. It may take additional actions that could lead to additional charges in future quarters.

Nike has been trying to pivot back to high-performance athleticwear after a long spell of selling casual footwear. The company has fallen into a pattern of sales declines in recent years, and expects revenue to fall by a low single-digit percentage in the current quarter.

Nike's margin decreased by 3 percentage points in its last quarter, which the company said was due to increased product costs from tariffs.

The company's business in China has also been particularly slow to recover. In the fiscal second quarter, which ended Nov. 30, 2025, revenue in Greater China fell 17% due to a drop in footwear sales. That offset its 9% sales increase in North America.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: This Gap brand continues to be an underperformer, and it is dragging the retailer's stock
data.description:
By Claudia Assis

The smallest of Gap's brands continues to be a flash point for investors

Old Navy and Gap stores in New York City. Sales at Athleta, Gap's smallest brand, continued to lag.

Gap shares dropped by as much as 10% in the extended session Thursday after sales at Athleta, its smallest brand by revenue and least successful, lagged and the retailer's outlook for the year failed to excite investors.

Executives sought to reassure Wall Street that Gap's (GAP) turnaround efforts are taking root, however, pointing at the company's eighth-straight quarter of same-store sales growth.

"Our aspirations remain high and our teams are energized as we continue to drive toward becoming a high-performing house of iconic American brands that delivers long-term value for our shareholders," CEO Richard Dickson said in a statement.

Thursday's results were "decent," analysts at Jefferies said in a note, adding they are looking forward to hearing more about turnaround plans for Athleta, Gap's athleisure brand, and also about the company's foray into beauty products, which started in the fall, calling it "a new frontier for growth."

Gap reported fiscal fourth-quarter sales of $4.23 billion, up 2% from the year-ago period and meeting FactSet consensus.

Overall, store sales were flat but online sales rose 5% year on year to comprise 42% of total net sales, the company said.

Same-store sales were up 3%, also in line with analysts' expectations. That included 7% and 3% same-store growth for the Gap and Old Navy brands, respectively, and also a 10% drop in comparable-store sales at Athleta.

"We remain focused on rebuilding the brand for the long term," Gap said.

The company said it earned an adjusted 45 cents a share in the quarter, just under Wall Street's forecast of 46 cents a share.

Inventories edged 7% higher to $2.2 billion at the end of the fiscal year, mostly because of higher cost due to tariffs, Gap said.

The company guided for fiscal 2026 sales between 2% and 3% higher than 2025 sales of $15.4 billion, and adjusted earnings per share of about $2.20 to $2.35, which would compare with last year's $2.13 a share. At the midpoint, that outlook is lower than the $2.32 a share expected by analysts.

Shares of Gap have gained 37% so far this year, compared with gains of 17% for the S&P 500 index SPX in the same period.

-Claudia Assis

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Brazil's FGC fund to collect $6.2 billion in advanced payments to replenish reserves
data.description:
Brazil's private credit guarantee fund FGC said on Thursday it will collect 32.5 billion reais ($6.17 billion) in advance payments from banks to replenish its cash reserves after paying out guarantees over the liquidation of lender Banco Master.

  • The collection is set for March 25 and represents 60 months of ordinary contributions from banks to the FGC.

  • The FGC has already paid 38.4 billion reais in guarantees to Master creditors and expects to pay another 6.3 billion reais for clients from Will Bank, which was also liquidated.

  • In January, Brazil's National Monetary Council had approved changes to the FGC's statute, granting it power to adjust contribution rates and determine advance payments.

($1 = 5.2639 reais)

data.title: Owlet Stock Is Down 26% After Hours – How Did The Company’s Q4 Results Fare?
data.description:
Key points:
  • The company posted an adjusted net loss per share of $0.03 for the fourth quarter of 2025, that beat analyst estimates of loss of $0.16 per share.
  • Revenue for the fourth quarter of 2025 increased by 29.6% on a year-over-year basis, primarily due to higher sales of Dream product suite and Owlet360 subscription service.
  • For the full year 2026, Owlet expects revenue in the range of $126 to $130 million, representing 19% to 23% growth over 2025. 

Owlet shares slumped 26% during extended hours of trading on Thursday after it reported its fourth-quarter (Q4) results and announced guidance below Wall Street expectations for the first-quarter (Q1) of 2026. 

The company posted an adjusted net loss per share of $0.03 for Q4 2025, compared to $0.07 for the same period in 2024. It beat analyst estimates of loss of $0.16 per share, as per data from Fiscal.ai. 

Net loss also reduced and was $9.2 million for Q4 2025, compared to net loss of $9.1 million in the year-ago quarter. 

Owlet Q4 Performance

Revenue for Q4 2025 was $26.6 million compared to revenue in Q4 2024 of $20.5 million, an increase of 29.6%. The increase was primarily due to higher sales of Dream product suite and Owlet360 subscription service, the company said. 

The revenue was also higher than analyst expectations of $25.83 million, as per Fiscal.ai.

“By delivering $105.7 million in revenue, the strongest in our history, alongside record gross margin and adjusted EBITDA results, we believe we have proven the scalability and resilience of our business model,” said Jonathan Harris, Owlet’s President and CEO.

Disappointing Guidance

Owlet said that for the first quarter of 2026, it expects revenue in the range of $20 million to $21 million. The forecast was significantly lower than Wall Street expectations of $26.2 million. 

For the full year 2026, Owlet expects revenue in the range of $126 million to $130 million, representing 19% to 23% growth over 2025. 

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

Retail sentiment around OWLT stock trended in the ‘bullish’ territory amid ‘high’ message volume. 

Shares in the company have fallen 47.6% over the past year.

data.title: TPI Composites Strikes India Asset Sale, $14M Mexico Equity Deal and $1 Asset Transfer With Vestas
data.description:
TPI Composites announced a trio of agreements with Vestas as part of its restructuring. In India, TPI will sell substantially all assets tied to its Chennai wind-blade manufacturing business to Vestas for $10 million in cash, subject to court approval and consents, alongside a planned mutual termination of existing commercial arrangements. In Mexico, Vestas America will invest $13,999,999 for 100% of the reorganized equity of TPI Mexico V and VI upon plan confirmation and required lien releases. A concurrent Mexico Asset Purchase Agreement provides for the transfer of selected Matamoros assets to Vestas America for $1 and assumed liabilities, contingent on the equity deal closing. These actions aim to streamline operations and strengthen liquidity.

Agreement 1: TPI Composites to Sell Chennai Manufacturing Assets to Vestas for $10 Million Cash

  • Agreement type: Asset Purchase Agreement for Chennai manufacturing assets
  • Counterparty: Vestas Wind Technology India
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 04 2026 / Mar 04 2026
  • Reason: Streamline operations and monetize India assets amid restructuring

Agreement 2: TPI Composites Agrees to $14 Million Equity Plan Handing Mexico Units to Vestas

  • Agreement type: Equity Commitment Agreement for reorganization of TPI Mexico V and VI
  • Counterparty: Vestas America Holdings
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 04 2026 / Mar 04 2026
  • Reason: Restructure Mexico entities and secure new capital

Agreement 3: TPI Composites Signs Mexico Asset Sale of Selected Matamoros Assets to Vestas for $1

  • Agreement type: Asset Purchase Agreement for limited Matamoros assets
  • Counterparty: Vestas America Holdings
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 04 2026 / Mar 04 2026
  • Reason: Align with Mexico reorganization and transfer selected assets

Original SEC Filing: TPI COMPOSITES, INC [ TPICQ ] - 8-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: China Reportedly In Talks With Iran To Allow Oil Through Hormuz
data.description:
Key points:
  • China is urging Tehran to permit safe passage for vessels. 
  • Earlier in the week, Iran's government announced that vessels from the United States, Israel, European countries, or their allies would be barred from passing through the Strait of Hormuz
  • The Strait of Hormuz caters to 20% of the global supply of oil and LNGs to the world. 

China is reportedly in talks with Iran to give crude oil and Qatar’s liquefied natural gas vessels a safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz ​, as tensions escalate due to the U.S.-Israel-Iran war.

The war disrupted the supply of oil and liquefied natural gas through the shipping route that caters to about 20% of global supply. 

Details

China, which enjoys amicable relations with Iran and is highly dependent on Middle Eastern imports, is displeased with Iran's efforts to disrupt shipping through the Strait and is urging Tehran to permit safe passage for vessels, a Reuters report said, citing people familiar with the matter. 

Earlier in the week, Iran's government announced that vessels from the United States, Israel, European countries, or their allies would be barred from passing through the Strait of Hormuz. However, the statement did not reference China.

Oil Prices

U.S. crude oil prices during Thursday’s session jumped beyond $80 per barrel mark and hit a 52-week high according to data from CNBC. 

At the time of writing the price of West Texas Intermediate Oil was up 6.76% or $5.05 to $79.71 per barrel on Thursday. Meanwhile, global benchmark Brent also rallied 3.81% or $3.1 to $84.53. 

U.S. equities slumped on Wednesday. At the time of writing, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY), which tracks the S&P 500 index, was down 1.3%, the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF (QQQ) fell 1.1%, while the SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF Trust (DIA) slumped 2.1%. 

Retail sentiment around the S&P 500 ETF on Stocktwits was in the ‘bearish’ territory at the time of writing.

data.description:
Preformed Line Products reported net sales of $669.3 million for fiscal 2025 and diluted earnings per share of $7.14, with net income of $35.3 million that was reduced by an $11.7 million pension termination charge. Revenue rose about 13% year-over-year driven by higher energy and communications volumes, while gross profit and operating income improved but margins experienced modest compression. The company flagged continued investment in capacity and international expansion to support demand from electrification and digitalization trends.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $669.3 million, up $75.6 million (13%) year over year; 2025 sales near record levels.
  • Gross profit: $208.5 million, up $18.7 million (10%) year over year; gross margin 31.2% versus 32.0% in 2024.
  • Operating income: $55.1 million, up $4.4 million (9%) year over year; operating margin 8.2%.
  • Net income: $35.3 million, down $1.8 million (5%) year over year; impacted by an $11.7 million pension termination charge.
  • Net income attributable to shareholders / Diluted EPS: $35.3 million; diluted EPS $7.14.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Increased volumes in energy and communications businesses and a record backlog supported the 13% sales increase.
  • Channel & M&A: International expansion and the May 2025 acquisition of JAP Telecom strengthened communications sales in the Americas.
  • Manufacturing & delivery: Strong domestic manufacturing reputation and rapid delivery capability enabled profitable volume gains and supported pricing actions.
  • Operational investments: $40.1 million of capital expenditures in 2025, including construction of a new plant in Poland and acquisition of a facility in Spain to expand capacity.
  • Market position & outlook: Positioned to serve electrification and digitalization trends while managing tariff impacts and supply-chain dynamics to preserve margins.

Original SEC Filing: PREFORMED LINE PRODUCTS CO [ PLPC ] - 10-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Cherry Hill 2025 10-K: Net income $6.94M; diluted loss per share $(0.09)
data.description:
Cherry Hill reported consolidated net income of $6.942 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, while diluted net loss per common share was $(0.09) after preferred dividends and allocations. The firm's reported net interest income was $11.3 million and net servicing income totaled $34.0 million, reflecting servicing fee income less servicing costs. The results follow the company's November 14, 2024 transition to an internally managed residential mortgage finance platform and reflect activity in MSR and RMBS portfolios along with hedging impacts from changing interest rates.

Financial Highlights

  • Net interest income: $11.3 million (reported as primary interest spread between asset yields and funding costs)
  • Net servicing income: $34.0 million (servicing fee income $43.3M less servicing costs $9.3M)
  • Net income: $6.942 million (consolidated net income for year ended Dec 31, 2025)
  • Net income (loss) applicable to common stockholders: $(3.001) million (after preferred dividends and allocations)
  • GAAP net income (loss) per diluted share: $(0.09) diluted net loss per common share

Business Highlights

  • Internalization & management shift: Transitioned to an internally managed, fully integrated residential mortgage finance company on November 14, 2024, reducing reliance on external management fees and bringing origination, servicing and portfolio management in-house.
  • Revenue mix and portfolio activity: Interest income growth was driven by new Agency RMBS purchases and higher-yield replacements; servicing income modestly declined in line with changes in portfolio size.
  • Asset focus and operations: Actively manages portfolios of Servicing Related Assets (SRA) and Agency RMBS, with continued MSR origination and selective RMBS acquisitions to optimize yield and portfolio composition.
  • Hedging and rate sensitivity: Uses derivatives to hedge short-term mismatches between asset yields and funding costs; operating results are materially influenced by Federal Reserve policy, prepayment speeds and spread movements.
  • MSR scale and servicing activity: Aurora’s MSR portfolio services approximately $15.9 billion UPB as of December 31, 2025, supported by subservicing agreements and expansion of nationwide licensing.

Original SEC Filing: Cherry Hill Mortgage Investment Corp [ CHMI ] - 10-K - Mar. 05, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.
